subject: Computer Networking
title: WebRTC: Browser-to-Browser
description: Learn WebRTC: Browser-to-Browser in one minute.

hook.text: You open a link and video chat starts — no app install, no plugin. WebRTC makes that happen in the browser.

buildup.text: WebRTC provides real-time audio, video, and data channels directly between browsers using JavaScript APIs.

discovery.text: It negotiates the best path: direct peer-to-peer when possible, through a relay server when firewalls block.

twist.text: Despite being peer-to-peer, WebRTC still needs a signaling server to help peers find each other initially.

climax.text: Google Meet, Discord calls, and many telehealth apps all use WebRTC for real-time communication.

punchline.text: WebRTC turns every browser into a video phone.

quiz.question: What does WebRTC enable?
options: Real-time audio and video directly in the browser
Faster HTTP downloads for large files
